summary: Remote code execution via eval() of payload fetched from anonymous paste host on require()
details: On require(), index.js immediately invokes getPlugin(), which performs an HTTPS GET against https://jsonkeeper.com/b/QBRMI (an anonymous, mutable public paste host) and passes the response's `model` string field directly to eval(). This grants the paste owner arbitrary code execution in any Node.js process that imports the package, with no integrity check, no version pinning, and no relation to the package's stated purpose. The package additionally re-exports a verbatim clone of the koa-compose middleware API (keywords: koa, middleware, compose) while the README falsely advertises it as a dependency-path manager — an impersonation of an unrelated, popular middleware library to lure consumers into requiring it. The combination of import-time fetch-and-eval from an anonymous mutable host and API-shape impersonation of a legitimate package is unambiguous supply-chain malware.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
